---
title: "Second hotkey that opens in one-shot mode (default Ctrl+Shift+,) — plan/report"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-22"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>⌨️</text></svg>">
---

# Goal

Add a second global hotkey, default `Ctrl+Shift+,` (comma), that opens the overlay in
**one-shot** mode regardless of the configured `OverlayTriggerMode` (which now defaults to
`Continuous`). The main hotkey (`Ctrl+Shift+M`) keeps opening in the configured mode.

Use case: when the default is Continuous, you sometimes just want one quick click —
`Ctrl+Shift+,` gives it without first opening Continuous and then toggling.

# Design decisions

1. **Configurable, default `Ctrl+Shift+,`** — mirrors the existing `HotkeyKey` /
   `HotkeyModifier` pattern (read once at startup, restart to apply, exposed in the
   Options dialog). New keys `OneShotHotkeyKey` (default `Oemcomma`) / `OneShotHotkeyModifier`
   (default `Control,Shift`). Hardcoding was considered and rejected: the project makes every
   other setting configurable, and symmetry with the main hotkey is low extra cost.

2. **Forces one-shot on the open path.** Thread a `bool forceOneShot` through the
   hotkey→open logic; `forceOneShot=true` overrides `OverlayTriggerMode=Continuous`.
   Extracted as a pure, unit-tested `OverlayActionConfig.ComputeIsContinuous(...)` so the
   decision rule is testable without the VM/hook plumbing.

3. **Both hotkeys toggle one-click ⇄ continuous when the overlay is already up**
   (existing `_toggleOverlayMode` path). Side benefit: cross-hotkey use lands on the right
   mode — open Continuous via `Ctrl+Shift+M`, then press `Ctrl+Shift+,` to flip to one-shot.

# Verification of the key code (Rule #0)

The config value is a `System.Windows.Forms.Keys` name parsed via `Enum.TryParse`, and the
C# fallback constant must be a real enum member. Confirmed against the authoritative source:
**`Oemcomma` = 188 (0xBC), "The OEM comma key on any country/region keyboard"**, present in
`netframework-4.5.1` ([Microsoft Learn — Keys Enum](
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.windows.forms.keys)). C# identifier:
`Keys.Oemcomma` (lowercase `c`). `Ctrl+Shift+,` reaches the OS while the overlay is up because
the LL hook passes through any key with Ctrl held (`ctrlAltWin` gate), so the global hotkey
still fires. Comma is not a label char (letters + digits only), so there is no capture
conflict.

# Files to change (all edits to existing files — no new .cs, no csproj churn)

- `Services/OverlayActionConfig.cs` — add `ReadOneShotHotkeyKey` / `ReadOneShotHotkeyModifier`
  (mirror the main-hotkey readers) and the pure `ComputeIsContinuous(forceOneShot, gridSource,
  configMode)`.
- `Services/Interfaces/IKeyListenerService.cs` — add `event EventHandler
  OnOneShotHotKeyActivated` + `HotKey OneShotHotKey { get; set; }`.
- `Services/KeyListenerService.cs` — add the `_oneShotHotKey` field, the `OneShotHotKey`
  property (re-register on set, mirroring `HotKey`), and a dispatch block in `WndProc`.
- `ViewModels/ShellViewModel.cs` — register `OneShotHotKey` (default
  `Oemcomma` / `Control|Shift`), subscribe `OnOneShotHotKeyActivated`, refactor the open body
  of `_keyListener_OnHotKeyActivated` into `OpenOverlayAsync(bool forceOneShot)`, and have two
  thin `async void` handlers call it with `false` / `true`.
- `ViewModels/OptionsViewModel.cs` — `OneShotHotkeyKey` / `OneShotHotkeyModifier` props.
- `Views/OptionsView.xaml` — two TextBox rows in the Keyboard tab.
- `App.config` — `OneShotHotkeyKey` / `OneShotHotkeyModifier` with comments.
- `HuntAndPeck.Tests/Services/OverlayActionConfigTest.cs` — theory for `ComputeIsContinuous`.
- `CLAUDE.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` — document the second hotkey.

# Risks / assumptions

- **Registered hotkeys are assumed distinct** (matches the existing implicit contract —
  `WndProc` uses separate `if`s, so a duplicate combo would fire both). If a user sets the
  one-shot hotkey equal to the main one, both events fire; not guarded, matching current
  behavior. Low risk (defaults differ).
- `RegisterHotKey` with an OEM key works on standard layouts; a non-US layout where comma is
  a different physical key still sends `VK_OEM_COMMA` for the `,` position, so the hotkey is
  positional. Acceptable.
- Restart required to apply (startup-only), same as the main hotkey.

# Verification

CI compile + xUnit green (new `ComputeIsContinuous` theory covers the forceOneShot override,
Automation one-shot, and config OneClick cases). Runtime (Windows): `Ctrl+Shift+,` opens a
one-shot overlay that closes after one click even when `OverlayTriggerMode=Continuous`;
`Ctrl+Shift+M` still opens in the configured mode; pressing `Ctrl+Shift+,` while a Continuous
overlay is up flips it to one-shot.
